# UC-22.13.15 · ESP Inbound First-Seen External Source Detection

## Description

We detect new external sources allowed through ESP entry rules so undocumented inbound paths are reviewed before they become normal traffic.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network and sourcetypes pan:traffic, pan:traffic`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Seed `esp_inbound_baseline.csv` with known vendor ranges. (2) Promote reviewed sources into the baseline. (3) Create ES notable for first-seen. (4) Map rules to EAP register fields. Indexes required: `index=network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, `sourcetype=pan:traffic``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, lookup `esp_inbound_baseline.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Seed `esp_inbound_baseline.csv` with known vendor ranges. (2) Promote reviewed sources into the baseline. (3) Create ES notable for first-seen. (4) Map rules to EAP register fields.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic dest_zone="ESP_DMZ" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| eval ext_src=if(cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",src) OR cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",src) OR cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",src),null(),src)
| where isnotnull(ext_src)
| lookup esp_inbound_baseline.csv ext_src AS ext_src OUTPUT approved_vendor
| where isnull(approved_vendor)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen values(dest) as targets by ext_src, rule
| sort - first_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**ESP Inbound First-Seen External Source Detection (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.3)** — We detect new external sources allowed through ESP entry rules so undocumented inbound paths are reviewed before they become normal traffic.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, lookup `esp_inbound_baseline.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ext_src** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(ext_src)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved_vendor)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ext_src, rule** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**ESP Inbound First-Seen External Source Detection (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.3)** — We detect new external sources allowed through ESP entry rules so undocumented inbound paths are reviewed before they become normal traffic.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, lookup `esp_inbound_baseline.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (new sources), Map (geo), Time chart (first-seen rate)

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic dest_zone="ESP_DMZ" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| eval ext_src=if(cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",src) OR cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",src) OR cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",src),null(),src)
| where isnotnull(ext_src)
| lookup esp_inbound_baseline.csv ext_src AS ext_src OUTPUT approved_vendor
| where isnull(approved_vendor)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen values(dest) as targets by ext_src, rule
| sort - first_seen
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (new sources), Map (geo), Time chart (first-seen rate). Key fields: `ext_src`, `first_seen`, `rule`, `targets`.
